taxonomy.title: FiDA and Related Laws
taxonomy.description: A classification system for the Financial Data Access (FiDA) regulation framework with embedded AI extraction logic and few-shot examples.

subcategories.name: EU
subcategories.description: In this subcategory, we include all FiDA related milestones that have been issued on EU level via the FiDA regulation, and related legal sources.

ai_extraction_instructions.extraction_scope: Clauses defining dates or time-bound obligations originating from EU-level bodies.
ai_extraction_instructions.disambiguation_logic: If the milestone refers to a member state's specific deadline, map to 'National'.
golden_examples: This Regulation shall enter into force on the twentieth day following that of its publication in the Official Journal.
Data holders shall make data available... 18 months after the entry into force of this Regulation.

subcategories.name: National
subcategories.description: In this subcategory, we include all FiDA related milestones that have been issued on the level of national jurisdiction.

ai_extraction_instructions.extraction_scope: Deadlines or timelines specific to member state implementation.
ai_extraction_instructions.disambiguation_logic: Must reference a specific country or 'Member States shall' in the context of local transposition.
golden_examples: Member States shall adopt and publish the laws, regulations and administrative provisions necessary to comply with this Directive by 17 January 2026.

subcategories.name: Local regulators
subcategories.description: In this subcategory, we include all FiDA related milestones that have been issued on the level of local regulators.

ai_extraction_instructions.extraction_scope: Dates set by specific National Competent Authorities (NCAs).
ai_extraction_instructions.disambiguation_logic: Focus on administrative deadlines set by the regulator.
golden_examples: The Competent Authority shall designate the Financial Data Sharing Schemes by 31 December 2025.

subcategories.name: Schemes
subcategories.description: In this subcategory, we include all FiDA related milestones that have been issued by schemes.

ai_extraction_instructions.extraction_scope: Operational deadlines set by a Financial Data Sharing Scheme (FDSS).
ai_extraction_instructions.disambiguation_logic: Contractual/operational milestones found in Scheme Rulebooks.
golden_examples: All Scheme Members must upgrade to API version 2.1 by 1st September.

subcategories.name: Scheme formation
subcategories.description: In this subcategory, we include all rules that define how schemes are created.

ai_extraction_instructions.extraction_scope: Rules for the genesis and authorization of the Scheme entity.
negative_constraints: Rules for companies joining an existing scheme
ai_extraction_instructions.disambiguation_logic: CREATING the club vs. JOINING the club.
golden_examples: Financial data sharing schemes shall be subject to authorization by the competent authority.
The application for authorization shall contain the governance modalities of the scheme.

subcategories.name: Scheme access
subcategories.description: In this subcategory, we include all rules that define how regulated companies enter a scheme.

ai_extraction_instructions.extraction_scope: Rules for Data Holders/Users to become members.
ai_extraction_instructions.disambiguation_logic: Gatekeeper rules—who gets in and how.
golden_examples: Data holders shall be obliged to join the financial data sharing scheme.
Membership in the scheme shall be open to all data users on non-discriminatory terms.

subcategories.name: Scheme management
subcategories.description: In this subcategory, we capture all obligations that the scheme must fulfil to manage and govern itself.

golden_examples: The scheme shall publish an annual report on its operations.
The scheme shall maintain a website listing all its members.

subcategories.name: Misc scheme
subcategories.description: In this subcategory, we include all rules about schemes that are not related to formation, access, or management.

golden_examples: In the event of dissolution, the scheme shall notify the competent authority.

subcategories.name: Dashboard
subcategories.description: Rules regarding the creation and operation of a customer dashboard.

golden_examples: Data holders shall provide customers with a permission dashboard to monitor their data sharing.
The dashboard shall allow the customer to view a history of all permissions granted.

subcategories.name: Data management
subcategories.description: In this subcategory, we include all rules governing how regulated companies must manage customer data.

golden_examples: Data users shall only process financial data for the purposes for which the customer has granted permission.
Data holders shall not use the data for advertising purposes without explicit consent.

subcategories.name: Data quality
subcategories.description: In this subcategory, we include all rules that define how regulated companies must manage the quality of customer data.

golden_examples: Data holders shall ensure that the data made available is accurate and up-to-date.
Customers shall have the right to request the correction of inaccurate data.

subcategories.name: Permission workflows
subcategories.description: Rules regarding notifications and interactions to grant or withdraw permission.

golden_examples: Data users shall obtain the explicit permission of the customer before accessing data.
The customer shall be able to withdraw permission at any time with the same ease as granting it.

subcategories.name: Data exchange
subcategories.description: In this subcategory, we include all rules that describe how regulated companies must exchange data on behalf of the customer.

golden_examples: Data holders shall make data available via a secure application programming interface (API).
The interface shall ensure a maximum latency of 200ms.

subcategories.name: Misc customer
subcategories.description: Rules directly related to interaction with the customer not covered elsewhere.

golden_examples: Financial information service providers shall establish efficient procedures for the handling of customer complaints.

subcategories.name: Governance & reporting
subcategories.description: Internal governance and reporting.

golden_examples: The management body shall define and oversee the implementation of the data governance strategy.
Regulated entities shall establish an independent internal audit function.

subcategories.name: Operational
subcategories.description: Business processes and IT systems supporting FiDA compliance.

golden_examples: Data holders shall have in place a business continuity plan to ensure the availability of data.
Appropriate technical and organizational measures shall be taken to ensure the security of data transmission.

subcategories.name: Official bodies
subcategories.description: Rules defined by official bodies and obligations to interact with them.

ai_extraction_instructions.disambiguation_logic: What you send them vs. Who they are.
golden_examples: Data holders shall report any major incident to the competent authority without undue delay.
Regulated entities shall provide the competent authority with all information necessary for supervision.

subcategories.name: Misc enforcement
subcategories.description: In this subcategory, we list all enforcement measures that are neither penalties nor administrative measures.

golden_examples: The authority may request any information necessary to verify compliance.

subcategories.name: Administrative measures
subcategories.description: This subcategory describes administrative measures that may be imposed on regulated companies.

ai_extraction_instructions.disambiguation_logic: Actions that restrict operations or name/shame.
golden_examples: The competent authority may issue an order requiring the natural or legal person to cease the conduct.
A public statement indicating the identity of the natural or legal person and the nature of the infringement.

subcategories.name: Penalties
subcategories.description: This subcategory describes penalties that may be imposed on regulated companies if they fail to comply.

golden_examples: Administrative fines of up to 2% of the total worldwide annual turnover.
Periodic penalty payments shall be imposed for each day of non-compliance.

subcategories.name: Regulated sectors
subcategories.description:
You are given a fixed list of 16 predefined regulated sector items.

Your task is to assign content from the source text to each of these items.

IMPORTANT:
- You must use exactly the item names as provided below.
- Do not create, modify, merge, split, or rename any items.
- Do not output any items that are not in the list.
- Treat the list as fixed labels, not as concepts to reinterpret.

For each item:
- Determine whether it is in scope based on the source text.
- Base your decision only on the text.
- If no relevant information is found, leave it empty or mark it as not in scope.

Items (fixed labels):
- Credit Institution
- Payment Institution
- Electronic Money Institution
- Investment Firm
- Manager of Alternative Investment Funds (AIFM)
- UCITS Management Company
- Credit Rating Agency
- Crowdfunding Service Provider
- Insurance Undertaking
- Reinsurance Undertaking
- Insurance Intermediary
- Ancillary Insurance Intermediary
- Institution for Occupational Retirement Provision (IORP)
- PEPP Provider
- Crypto-Asset Service Provider (CASP)
- Issuer of Asset-Referenced Tokens

Before answering, verify that your output contains only these exact item names.

golden_examples: This Regulation applies to the following entities: credit institutions, payment institutions...

subcategories.name: Included data
subcategories.description: Which data falls within the scope.

golden_examples: This Regulation applies to the following categories of data: data on mortgage credit agreements, loans, and accounts.

subcategories.name: Excluded data
subcategories.description: Data categories explicitly excluded from scope.

golden_examples: This Regulation shall not apply to data related to sickness and health insurance.

subcategories.name: Misc scope
subcategories.description: Rules delineating scope not covered by sectors or data.

golden_examples: This Regulation applies to data holders established in the Union.

subcategories.name: Definitions
subcategories.description: Terms explicitly defined in the FiDA regulation.

golden_examples: 'Customer' means a natural or legal person who makes use of a financial product.

subcategories.name: Roles
subcategories.description: Descriptions of data holder, data user, and FISP.

ai_extraction_instructions.extraction_scope: Definitions of the three main actors.
golden_examples: 'Data holder' means a financial institution that collects, stores and otherwise processes customer data.

subcategories.name: Official bodies
subcategories.description: Descriptions of official bodies.

ai_extraction_instructions.disambiguation_logic: Who they are vs. What you send.
golden_examples: The European Banking Authority (EBA) is an independent EU authority which works to ensure effective and consistent prudential regulation.

subcategories.name: EU FiDA
subcategories.description: The FiDA regulation proposal itself (COM(2023) 360 final). This subcategory contains only the Financial Data Access Regulation.

ai_extraction_instructions.extraction_scope: The FiDA regulation proposal document only.
ai_extraction_instructions.disambiguation_logic: Only the FiDA regulation proposal belongs here. All other EU-level laws go to 'other_laws'.
golden_examples: Proposal for a Regulation of the European Parliament and of the Council on a framework for Financial Data Access (FiDA).

subcategories.name: Member state
subcategories.description: Legislation of member states.

golden_examples: German Payment Services Supervision Act (ZAG).

subcategories.name: Schemes
subcategories.description: Legal sources issued at scheme level.

golden_examples: SEPA Payment Account Access Scheme Rulebook v1.0.

subcategories.name: Other laws
subcategories.description: List of other documents used.

golden_examples: Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation).
